description:
Fix misleading error messages in the CLI status and doctor commands. When `opentabs status --port <port>` hits a non-OpenTabs HTTP server, the error says 'The server may be misconfigured' which is misleading — the port isn't running OpenTabs at all. The doctor command's MCP client config hint includes CWD-relative paths that resolve to root (/) in containers/headless environments, producing confusing output like '/.cursor/mcp.json'.

Known LOW issues (not addressed): plugin search shows empty description for packages without one; no config unset command; logs --lines N may return N-1 lines due to trailing newline.

userStories.title: Improve status error when port hosts a non-OpenTabs HTTP server
userStories.description: As a user running `opentabs status --port <port>` against a port that hosts a different HTTP server (e.g., Next.js), I want a clear error message saying the port is not running an OpenTabs server, so that I don't waste time debugging a 'misconfigured' server that doesn't exist.
acceptanceCriteria: When the /health endpoint returns a non-2xx status (e.g., 404 from a non-OpenTabs server), the error message says something like 'No OpenTabs server found on port <port>. The port may be in use by another service.' instead of 'The server may be misconfigured'
When the /health response is valid JSON but not a recognized health response (missing 'status' field), the message also indicates it's not an OpenTabs server
The existing 401 authentication error handling is unchanged
The connection refused (server not running) error handling is unchanged
userStories.notes: File: platform/cli/src/commands/status.ts, around line 76-79. Currently the catch for non-ok responses is a generic 'Error: MCP server returned HTTP ${res.status}' with 'The server may be misconfigured'. Change the message for 4xx responses (especially 404) to indicate the port likely isn't running OpenTabs. A good heuristic: if the response status is 404, say 'No OpenTabs server found on port ${port}. The port may be in use by another service.' If 5xx, keep the 'misconfigured' hint. Also consider checking the Content-Type header — a non-JSON response (text/html from Next.js) strongly indicates it's not OpenTabs.

userStories.title: Filter CWD-relative paths from doctor MCP client config hint
userStories.description: As a user running `opentabs doctor` in a container or from root directory, I want the MCP client config hint to only show useful paths, so that I'm not confused by entries like '/.cursor/mcp.json' or '/opencode.json' that resolve to filesystem root.
acceptanceCriteria: The doctor MCP client config check no longer includes CWD-relative paths (e.g., join(process.cwd(), '.cursor', 'mcp.json')) when CWD is the filesystem root ('/')
Alternatively: CWD-relative paths are only included when the CWD appears to be a project directory (e.g., has a package.json or .git directory)
The hint text that lists checked paths omits any CWD-relative paths that were skipped
Home-directory-relative paths (Claude Code, Windsurf) are always included regardless of CWD
userStories.notes: File: platform/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts, around line 230-237. The `defaultMcpClientLocations()` function includes CWD-relative paths for Cursor and OpenCode. These produce confusing output when CWD is '/' (common in Docker containers). Two approaches: (1) Only include CWD-relative paths if CWD is not the filesystem root AND CWD looks like a project (has package.json or .git). (2) Always include them but when displaying the hint, skip any path whose parent is '/'. Approach 1 is cleaner. The check function `checkMcpClientConfig` at line 239 iterates through clients — it already skips non-existent files, so the main issue is the hint text at line 266-272 which lists ALL checked paths. A CWD guard in `defaultMcpClientLocations()` is the simplest fix.
